title: Fix enum range constraints missing for rand variables in sub-objects (#7230)
body:
## Summary

Enum-typed `rand` variables inside `globalConstrained` sub-objects were missing the SMT hard constraint that restricts them to valid enum members. This caused the solver to treat them as unconstrained integers, producing invalid enum values.

## Issue

Fixes #7230

## Changes

- `src/V3Randomize.cpp`: Extracted `emitEnumConstraint` lambda from inline enum constraint logic; added recursive traversal of `globalConstrained` sub-objects to emit enum range constraints with path-prefixed SMT names (e.g., `inner.color`, `mid.inner.state`)

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_randomize_subobj_enum.{v,py}` — verifies enum validity for one-level and two-level sub-object nesting with non-contiguous enum values

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
